sections.paragraph:
φιάλη, ης, ἡ [orig. unclear] in the ancient Mediterranean world φ. refers to a dish or pan of adequate depth and circumference for holding or dispensing a commodity, such as food, liquid, medicine, magic potion, and esp. cultic offering, bowl, pan

—a. spec. of an offering bowl Rv 5:8.

—b. as a container used to dispense apocalyptic plagues or disasters Rv 15:7; 16:1 and oft. in Rv.
